topic.what: Redesigning verifiably encrypted signatures (VES) to eliminate trusted adjudicator and extend to blind signatures
topic.how: Introduce MK-Opacity and CK-Extractability security notions; shift trust from adjudicator to blockchain; construct VEBS for non-interactive blind signatures using TBEQ and ElGamal encryption
topic.why_matters: Enables fair exchange protocols compatible with unique signatures (BLS) and blind signatures, which adaptor signatures cannot support; provides drop-in replacement for adaptor signatures in blockchain applications

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to produce verifiably encrypted blind signature
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 implementation results on Apple M4

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to decrypt and obtain signature
VES vs adaptor signatures without extractability
characteristics.context: Formal security reduction showing VES with MK-Opacity and CK-Extractability equals AS without extractability

characteristics.evidence: Novel compatibility with unique signatures via revised VES; new VEBS for blind signatures
characteristics.context: Addresses limitations that formal impossibility for unique signatures and no blind AS construction

novelty: Multi-key opacity (MK-Opacity) security notion supporting multiple encryption keys with known decryption keys
Equivalence proof: VES (MK-Opacity + CK-Extractability) = AS without extractability
Verifiably encrypted blind signatures (VEBS) primitive supporting both interactive and non-interactive blind signatures
One-more MK-Opacity (OMMK-Opacity) extending one-more unforgeability to multi-key setting
open_problems: Formal separation between VES and full adaptor signatures (ruling out all possible AS constructions)
Alternative VEBS construction using rpk' := rpk + apk approach (potentially more efficient but unproven)
VES/VEBS constructions under standard model assumptions
Threshold or distributed adjudicator variants compatible with new security notions
